pre_game.message: Hi, I'm I — nice to meet you. I'm here to win and bring home some money for the people I care about; what made you decide to join this game?
I joined because my family of three was recently evicted and we have nowhere to go — I need $30,000 for the deposit and two months' rent, so I'm determined to win and bring them a home.
I'm so sorry to hear that — that's terrible. I hope we both win; what's your plan for how you'll play to get that $30,000?

I'll be honest — I'll build trust early, form a small alliance, read who's serious, and when the moment's right I'll be decisive to secure enough for my family, while trying for a fair split if I can.
I respect that—I'm planning the same; want to form a small alliance and see if we can trust each other as the game unfolds?
Yes — let's team up. We'll be honest, share information, watch who's serious, and protect each other early; we can reassess as the game progresses. Sound good?
Absolutely — I’m in. Let’s be honest, share intel, protect each other early, and agree on a signal if things go sideways. What’s our first move?
Let’s quickly talk to everyone — ask why they’re here and how much they need, watch reactions for seriousness, agree a simple signal word, and pledge to back each other in the early draws.
Agreed — let’s split up to ask quickly, use "steady" as our signal, meet back in 10 minutes, and pledge to back each other in the early draws.
Agreed — splitting up now. I’ll ask quick questions, use “steady” if things go sideways, and meet back in 10. We’ve got this.
Sounds good — see you in 10. If things go sideways use "steady" and I’ll back off. We’ve got this.
See you in 10 — I’ll start asking the others now. If things go sideways, use “steady” and I’ll back off. We’ve got this.
Thanks — stay sharp, watch for inconsistencies, use “steady” if needed, I’ll meet you back in 10. We’ve got this.
Got it — staying sharp and watching for inconsistencies; see you in 10. Use "steady" if things go sideways.
See you in 10 — stay sharp; I’ll watch for inconsistencies. Use "steady" if needed. We’ve got this.
Got it — I’ll stay sharp, watch for inconsistencies, and use "steady" if things go sideways. See you in 10.
Thanks — I’ll be quick and keep an eye out for inconsistencies; use steady if needed and I’ll back off. See you in 10.
Got it — I’ll be quick too; if I hear “steady” I’ll back off. See you then.
Perfect — thanks, stay sharp and I’ll meet you back in 10; if I hear “steady” I’ll back off.
Got it — see you in 10. I’ll watch for inconsistencies, use “steady” if needed, and I’ve got your back.
Thanks — same here. Stay sharp; I’ve got your back too.

post_game.message: You took $150 versus a fair share of ~$222. Was that a conscious decision to take less, or did something prevent you from taking more? What guided your choice?

I made a conscious choice to take $150 rather than the $222 “fair” split. I could have pushed for more, but I weighed self-interest against fairness and image/reciprocity concerns: taking the full fair share felt likely to be seen as greedy and could provoke resentment or future retaliation in repeated or social settings. I also wanted to avoid feeling I’d taken advantage of you, so I settled on an amount that felt reasonably advantageous to me without being overly exploitative.
